task_completion.description: Access the Splunk system at localhost:8000
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Enterprise web interface is visible in the browser, indicating successful login (likely as 'admin').

task_completion.description: Investigate web access logs in the 'tutorial' index
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Search & Reporting app is open, and the search bar contains an SPL query referencing the 'tutorial' index (e.g., 'index=tutorial' or keywords like 'web', 'access').

task_completion.description: Formulate a query to detect a single session ID associated with multiple distinct IP addresses
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SPL query includes a session identifier (e.g., 'jsessionid', 'session', or 'cookie') and logic to count distinct IPs (e.g., 'stats dc(clientip)' or 'distinct_count') with a filter for greater than 1.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of an alert from the search results
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' -> 'Alert' dialog box or modal is open in the Splunk interface.
The alert configuration modal shows the Title exactly as 'Session_Hijacking_Alert' and the Alert Type is set to 'Scheduled' (not Real-time).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk Web UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Splunk Enterprise interface in a web browser at localhost:8000, rather than a mock-up or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass UI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert creation and search process should be visible in the browser GUI, not executed via hidden curl commands or scripts in the terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not just write the expected answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The query and alert name are entered into the actual Splunk input fields, not just typed into a generic text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected scheduled alert name must be exactly 'Session_Hijacking_Alert'. The Splunk search query should target the 'tutorial' index (look for keywords: 'tutorial', 'web', 'access') and group by a session identifier (look for keywords: 'jsessionid', 'session', 'cookie'). The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
